title: aetheorhiza bulbosa subsp. montana
intro: Aetheorhiza bulbosa subsp. montana, also known as Mountain Aethorhiza, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive bulbous root system. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Mountain Aethorhiza include its potential in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
